sourceMovie.title: Paddington
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Paddington (2014) stuck because it commits to its comedy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Paul King's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The comedy and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: One Hundred and One Dalmatians
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Paddington clicked for you, One Hundred and One Dalmatians (1961) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and adventure, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Flushed Away
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Paddington clicked for you, Flushed Away (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and adventure, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Jungle Book

faq.question: What should I watch after Paddington?
faq.answer: Try One Hundred and One Dalmatians, Flushed Away, Ferdinand—each shares DNA with Paddington while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Paddington?
faq.answer: Paddington (2014) is best described as Comedy, Adventure, Family. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Paddington part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Paddington Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
